senses.definition: a short, flexible staff used for sword fighting and drill, or a stick used in singing contests (less common).
examples: The fencer practiced with a singlestick on the training mat.
In the old drill, cadets used a singlestick as part of their weapons training.

senses.definition: a person or thing that sticks alone or is singular in appearance; a coinage unlikely to be widely used.
examples: The term singlesticker is not common in modern English and may appear in niche contexts.
As a coinage, singlesticker has little real usage.

senses.definition: plural of singlestick; short, flexible fighting sticks used in historical martial arts.
examples: Historical manuals describe techniques with singlesticks.
Competitors practiced with singlesticks in the (now nostalgic) drill sessions.

senses.definition: a sleeveless undergarment worn as an outer shirt in some contexts; a single layer worn under clothes.
examples: He wore a white singlet under his shirt.
The gym outfit included a bright red singlet.

senses.definition: a thing that exists as a single instance; in programming, an object that is the only instance of its class.
examples: In the design pattern, a singleton ensures only one instance is created.
The dataset includes a singleton value that stands alone.

senses.definition: plural of singleton; multiple singletons (instances) in various contexts.
examples: The library stores several singletons in different modules.
In user data, there are multiple singleton entries for this key.

senses.definition: a narrow, single-lane trail or path, often for bikes or hikers.
examples: The mountain bike route followed a dusty singletrack through pines.
They rode the singletrack with care to avoid obstacles.

senses.definition: plural of singletrack; multiple narrow trails.
examples: The bike park offers many singletracks of varying difficulty.
They explored several singletracks during the weekend.

senses.definition: a bar attached to a horse's harness from which the traces of a cart or carriage extend; also called a whippletree.
examples: The farmer attached the singletree to the cart.
Harnesses and singletrees were common on old farm equipment.

senses.definition: plural of singletree; multiple bars used in horse-drawn equipment.
examples: Old carriages used several singletrees in their harness systems.
The cart had two singletrees extending to the traces.

senses.definition: plural of singlet; sleeveless undershirt or athletic top.
examples: She wore a blue singlet to the gym.
The team issued matching singlets for the event.

senses.definition: the act of selecting one item from a group; the process of concentrating on a single item.
examples: The teacher was singling students for individual attention.
In the experiment, singling out the variable helped reduce noise.

senses.definition: plural of singling; instances of singling out.
examples: The notes included several singlings for emphasis.
He avoided singlings that might embarrass anyone.

senses.definition: one at a time; alone; not together with others.
examples: The boxes were shipped singly to avoid damage.
He walked singly along the path.

senses.definition: a member of the Singpho people or their language, an ethnic group in parts of Northeast India and Myanmar.
examples: The Singpho language has unique tonal patterns.
Researchers studied Singpho cultural practices.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of sing; to produce musical sounds with the voice.
examples: She sings beautifully.
They sing together every Friday.

senses.definition: a playful reduplicative term for singing; sometimes used as a nickname or exclamation; or a diminutive form in some languages.
examples: The kids joked around, singing 'singsing' as they played.
In some contexts, 'singsing' can be a friendly nickname.

senses.definition: a song-like tune or a melodic, often sentimental, repetition of singing; a singsong voice or cadence.
examples: The lullaby had a gentle singsong rhythm.
Her voice had a singsong quality that calmed the children.

senses.definition: characteristic of a singsong; spoken with a rising and falling melody.
examples: He spoke in a singsong voice that sounded amused.
The register used a singsong cadence.

senses.definition: past tense of singsong; to sing in a singsong way.
examples: She singsonged a lullaby to calm the baby.
He speaks with a singsonged cadence when nervous.

senses.definition: the act or habit of speaking or singing in a singsong manner.
examples: Her singsonging made it hard to take the instructions seriously.
The teacher announced the rule against singsonging in class.

senses.definition: plural of singsong; songs or tunes that are sung in a singsong manner.
examples: The kindergarten sang several cheerful singsongs.
The album features traditional folk singsongs.

senses.definition: having the quality of a singsong; with a lilting or musical cadence.
examples: Her voice was singsongy, making the tale feel fairy-tale-ish.
They spoke in a singsongy rhythm that amused the listeners.

senses.definition: a form of German light opera with spoken dialogue and songs.
examples: The university theater produced a classic singspiel with witty dialogue.
Singspiel blends music and speech in a comic opera style.

senses.definition: plural of singspiel; multiple such works or performances.
examples: The festival featured several singspiels by local composers.
Critics praised the energy of the singspiels.

senses.definition: a female singer; a female vocalist.
examples: The famous opera singstress performed impeccably.
The concert featured several renowned singstresses.
